meta.title: South Korea vs Ghana — Group Stage

context: A pivotal moment in this group stage match. South Korea trail Ghana 2-3. Something has to change. The manager must decide how to respond.

options.label: A
options.action: Double substitution — inject fresh legs into attack and midfield

options.label: B
options.action: Switch to a back 3, push full-backs forward for overloads

options.label: C
options.action: Hold shape, trust the system, make changes at 75th minute

options.label: D
options.action: Triple substitution — all-out attacking change, gamble everything

real_decision.description: Ghana won 3-2. Mohamed Salisu (23'), Mohammed Kudus (33'), Gue-Sung Cho (57'), Gue-Sung Cho (60'), Mohammed Kudus (67').

pre_generated_explanations.A:
**OPTION A — Double substitution — inject fresh legs into attack and midfield**
Risk level: High. This is the proactive choice. Fresh legs can exploit tiring defenders and create new attacking patterns. In the context of this group stage match between South Korea and Ghana (2-3), this option addresses the tactical challenge but requires precise execution from the players on the pitch.
pre_generated_explanations.B:
**OPTION B — Switch to a back 3, push full-backs forward for overloads**
Risk level: Medium. This is the structural change. A back 3 gives more attacking width but requires the midfield to cover more ground. In the context of this group stage match between South Korea and Ghana (2-3), this option addresses the tactical challenge but requires precise execution from the players on the pitch.
pre_generated_explanations.C:
**OPTION C — Hold shape, trust the system, make changes at 75th minute**
Risk level: Medium. This is the patient approach. Trusting the system avoids panic but may waste precious time on the clock. In the context of this group stage match between South Korea and Ghana (2-3), this option addresses the tactical challenge but requires precise execution from the players on the pitch.
pre_generated_explanations.D:
**OPTION D — Triple substitution — all-out attacking change, gamble everything**
Risk level: Very High. This is the desperate gamble. Maximum attacking intent but leaves the team dangerously exposed to counter-attacks. In the context of this group stage match between South Korea and Ghana (2-3), this option addresses the tactical challenge but requires precise execution from the players on the pitch.
